pilotBudget.description: The Green Wallet operates within a fixed pilot budget of $15,000 over 12 months, teaching students about budget-constrained incentive design.

budgetLesson.title: Why Budget Caps Matter
budgetLesson.description: The Green Wallet teaches a fundamental principle of public finance: every incentive program operates within a fixed budget. Just like the City of Cerritos manages a $131.4M operating budget across 7 departments, the Green Wallet has a pilot budget of $15,000. Students learn to design rewards that maximize participation without exceeding the cap — the same challenge faced by real program managers.
keyInsights: A $15,000 pilot budget at $3 average reward = 5,000 verified actions maximum
If 200 students participate, that's 25 actions per student over the pilot
Cost per verified action ($3) is lower than many comparable municipal programs
Budget-neutral scaling requires sponsorship revenue to match incentive costs
